name: Kisspeptin
subtitle: KISS1 Gene Product | Reproductive Neuropeptide

modifications: C-terminally amidated peptides from 145 amino acid KISS1 precursor

overview: Kisspeptin acts as a master regulator of the reproductive system, stimulating GnRH neurons essential for puberty, fertility, and reproductive function.
mechanism: Binds to GPR54/KISS1R receptors on hypothalamic GnRH neurons, triggering pulsatile GnRH release, which stimulates pituitary LH/FSH secretion and gonadal steroid production.

items.name: Hypogonadotropic Hypogonadism
items.description: Restores reproductive hormone secretion in functional or congenital cases by stimulating dormant GnRH axis.

items.name: IVF Ovulation Trigger
items.description: Triggers oocyte maturation with 45% live birth rate and zero severe OHSS cases; safer than hCG.

items.name: Hypothalamic Amenorrhea
items.description: Restores reproductive hormone pulsatility in women with hypothalamic amenorrhea.

items.name: Hypoactive Sexual Desire Disorder (Men)
items.description: Modulates sexual brain processing; increases penile tumescence by 56% vs placebo.

items.name: Hypoactive Sexual Desire Disorder (Women)
items.description: Modulates sexual and attraction brain processing; increases self-reported sexiness.

items.name: Energy Metabolism
items.description: Preclinical evidence suggests influence on energy expenditure and activity levels.

deliveryMethods.overview: Standard administration route; allows precise dosing and rapid hormone stimulation.

steps: Clean work area and hands thoroughly with alcohol
Inject slowly down vial side (not directly onto powder)
Use within 7 days for KP-10; 14 days for KP-54

deliveryMethods.overview: Emerging non-invasive delivery method; 2025 research demonstrates effectiveness with stable formulations.

interactions.notes: Kisspeptin stimulates GnRH release; works upstream in reproductive axis.
Serves as potentially safer alternative for IVF ovulation triggering.
Restores natural testosterone production through LH stimulation.
Kisspeptin function modulated by sex steroids in feedback loops.

timeline.effects: Testosterone/estradiol increase in response to LH
Potential improvements in sexual function and libido

stopSigns: Ovarian hyperstimulation signs: severe pelvic pain, bloating, nausea
contraindications: Not recommended during pregnancy or breastfeeding
monitoring: Monitor for ovarian hyperstimulation in women
Consult reproductive endocrinologist for fertility use

good: White, fluffy powder; proper freeze-drying indicated
Clear solution after reconstitution; no particles or cloudiness
Protected from light in amber/opaque vials
warning: Slight compaction acceptable if dissolves completely with gentle swirling
bad: Cloudy after reconstitution indicates degradation or contamination

references.title: Effects of Kisspeptin on Sexual Brain Processing and Penile Tumescence in Men With Hypoactive Sexual Desire Disorder: A Randomized Clinical Trial
references.authors: Mills EG, Sheridan H, Bhushan S, et al. (Dhillo WS senior author)
references.keyFindings: 32 men completed trial. Kisspeptin-54 IV (1 nmol/kg/h for 75 min) significantly modulated sexual brain processing and increased penile tumescence by up to 56% vs placebo. Potential as first pharmacological treatment for male HSDD.

references.title: Effects of Kisspeptin Administration in Women With Hypoactive Sexual Desire Disorder: A Randomized Clinical Trial
references.authors: Sheridan H, Mills EG, Sherwood RA, et al. (Jayasena CD, Dhillo WS senior authors)
references.keyFindings: Double-masked, placebo-controlled, 2-way crossover trial in women with HSDD. Kisspeptin modulated sexual and facial attraction brain processing, with hippocampal activation correlating with distress and reduced sexual aversion.

references.title: Efficacy of Kisspeptin-54 to Trigger Oocyte Maturation in Women at High Risk of OHSS During IVF Therapy
references.authors: Jayasena CN, Abbara A, Comninos AN, et al. (Dhillo WS senior author)
references.keyFindings: Live birth rates of 45% overall per transfer; 62% at optimal 9.6 nmol/kg dose. Zero cases of moderate, severe, or critical OHSS. Oocyte maturation in 95% of women.

references.title: Intranasal Kisspeptin Administration Rapidly Stimulates Gonadotropin Release in Humans
references.authors: Sheridan H, Sherwood RA, Sheridan C, et al. (Dhillo WS senior author)
references.keyFindings: Intranasal kisspeptin-54 (12.8 nmol/kg) rapidly stimulated LH release in healthy men (+4.4 IU/L), healthy women (+1.4 IU/L), and women with hypothalamic amenorrhea (+4.4 IU/L). Formulation stable for 60 days at 4°C.

latestResearch.title: Kisspeptin and Neurokinin B: Roles in Reproductive Health
latestResearch.summary: Comprehensive review of kisspeptin's role as the natural inductor of ovulation, with expanding research into IVF trigger use and management of reproductive disorders.

latestResearch.title: Kisspeptins Regulating Fertility: Potential Future Therapeutic Approach in Infertility Treatment
latestResearch.summary: Exogenous kisspeptins show significant promise in restoring fertility, with studies advancing in ovarian stimulation protocols and cryoprotection during vitrification.

quickStats.frequency: Single dose for testing, or 2-3 times weekly (NEVER daily - causes desensitization)
quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: Room temperature. Reconstituted: 2-8°C. KP-10: use within 7 days. KP-54: use within 14 days
